summary: Set 047 Session 6 closes the v4 state-file schema migration session set with the documentation revision (canonical schema doc + authoring-guide update + set change-log), version bumps for the dual publish (PyPI dabbler-ai-router 0.8.0 → 0.9.0; Marketplace extension 0.21.0 → 0.22.0; ai_router/__init__.py __version__ string brought current from a stale 0.5.1), CLAUDE.md version-walk update + retargeted parking pointer (Set 048+ → Set 049 for the orchestrator-coordination-removal work), and a cross-provider verification round (gpt-5-4 tier 3, 873s, $0.505) that returned ISSUES_FOUND with 3 Critical + 4 Important + 2 Nice-to-have items — all 9 dispositioned in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope. Pre-emptively caught Critical 1 (per-session orchestrator close-out documented backwards) before the verifier returned by re-reading the S4 close-reason; the verifier's subsequent flag confirmed the catch and the in-flight rewrite of the field-table entry, Check-out/check-in section, rule 8, Lightweight tier worked example, three worked examples, and Tier expectations all landed in the right shape. Reader contract split into Layer 1 (normalize shim returning normalized dict with derived top-level fields), Layer 2 (get_progress/ProgressView with progress-counting fields only), Layer 3 (readCancellationState carve-out reading raw state.status). Derived top-level field semantics corrected to match progress.py lines 470-572 (startedAt: in-progress else most-recently-completed reversed; completedAt: ONLY when set-status==complete; lifecycleState: synthesized for in-progress and complete but NOT cancelled). verificationVerdict field type widened from strict 2-token enum to string|null to match writer behavior + the bundled S4 ISSUES_FOUND_RESOLVED_IN_FLIGHT extension token. v3-shim support horizon reworded from 'permanent' to 'persists through the transition' per spec §3.4 future-removal schedule. lastActivityAt bump claim narrowed to register_session_start only (close_session does NOT bump). Status vocabulary alignment caveat added (top-level allows cancelled, per-session does not). Build artifacts: dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.22.0.vsix built locally (23 files, 852.81 KB); PyPI release build NOT executed. Dual publish held by operator at close-out — option preserved to bundle with Set 048 (Lightweight parity) or Set 049 (orchestrator-coordination removal). Cumulative routed cost S1-S6: $2.31427 of $10 NTE (23.1%); plenty of headroom remained at set close.

reason.specifics: Set 047 is now fully closed (all 6 sessions complete); next session lives in a different set entirely. The two queued follow-on sets are Set 048 (Lightweight tier parity — audit-pending under its own S1 per the audit-then-spec discipline) and Set 049 (orchestrator-coordination removal — also audit-pending). Operator chooses which to start next; the next-orchestrator block here is a placeholder for the close-out gate's schema requirement, not a directive to continue this set.
